content: The investigation into the alleged bribery case involving Mr. Ramesh Kumar reveals the following: 1. The complainant, Mr. Arjun Mehta, provided a verifiable WhatsApp and call record of the bribe demand. 2. The trap was conducted in compliance with ACB guidelines — marked currency was prepared, witnesses were present, and the recovery was immediate. 3. However, forensic analysis of the recovered currency revealed that the notes were not from the ACB’s marked ledger, but from a bank withdrawal by Vikram Kumar on 11.10.2023. 4. Witness Priya Sharma’s claim of a third-party handover is contradicted by CCTV footage. 5. The recovery report initially stated that money was taken from the accused’s person, but internal logs show it was retrieved from his desk drawer after arrest. 6. Mr. Ramesh Kumar’s bank statements show no significant income, and his daughter is undergoing treatment for a chronic kidney condition at Max Hospital, Ghaziabad, with monthly expenses exceeding ₹45,000. 7. Mr. Vikram Kumar, cousin of the accused, has no criminal record but was seen near the GMC office on 12.10.2023 (confirmed by auto-rickshaw driver). 8. Conclusion: While the bribe transaction occurred, the ACB’s procedural documentation contains material inaccuracies. The accused did receive the money, but the notes were not the ones issued by ACB. The witness testimony of Priya Sharma appears coached. The motive is credible. Recommend prosecution under Section 7 of PCA, 1988, with note on procedural irregularities.
